response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with strong testing/TDD discipline and broad polyglot experience, but the resume shows minimal recent Java/Kotlin work and no demonstrated depth in the Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate ecosystem or IDE internals. Java appears only in passing (robotics, mobile orchestration) nearly a decade ago, and the JVM tooling focus of this role requires deeper, more current expertise than the resume evidences.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing tooling authorship (Fluent-Asserts, Trial)
Relevant IDE/tooling exposure via C/C++ work on NI LabWindows/CVI and authoring developer-experience frameworks
Excellent communication evidenced by workshops, talks, and teaching (Selenium course, Web Internals)
concerns: Very limited and dated Java experience; no Kotlin experience listed
No demonstrated work with Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or application server integration
Primary recent stack is TypeScript/Node and DLang, not JVM — significant ramp-up required for JVM internals work
